title: GLUTAMINE
description: Glutamine is the most abundant free amino acid in the human body, serving as a critical fuel source for immune cells and enterocytes in the digestive tract. While categorized as a non-essential amino acid, it becomes 'conditionally essential' during periods of metabolic stress, such as intense exercise, injury, or illness, where demand exceeds the body's synthesis capacity.

research.title: Glutamine in Burn Injury
research.summary: Clinical data indicates that glutamine supplementation in burn patients significantly reduces mortality, hospital stay length, and occurrences of gram-negative bacteremia by preserving gut barrier function.

research.title: Glutamine: a potentially useful supplement for athletes
research.summary: This review suggests that exogenous glutamine may help maintain muscle protein mass and support immune function in athletes undergoing heavy exercise training.

research.title: Impact of oral nutritional supplement composition on healing of different chronic wounds: A systematic review
research.summary: The study found that hyperproteic oral formulas containing glutamine and other nutrients can reduce wound area and increase healing rates in patients with pressure injuries and diabetic foot ulcers.

research.title: Randomised placebo-controlled trial of dietary glutamine supplements for postinfectious irritable bowel syndrome
research.summary: Oral glutamine supplementation was shown to safely and effectively reduce symptoms in patients suffering from post-infectious irritable bowel syndrome.

research.title: The effect of glutamine supplementation on athletic performance, body composition, and immune function: A systematic review and a meta-analysis of clinical trials
research.summary: A comprehensive meta-analysis found that glutamine supplementation reduces muscle soreness and improves recovery following intense exercise, though its effect on performance metrics is variable.

sentiment.summary: Users overwhelmingly report positive results for gut health and recovery from intense exercise, with many considering it a staple for managing IBS symptoms and 'leaky gut'.

effects.label: Gastrointestinal Relief:
effects.description: Highly effective for post-infectious IBS and general gut permeability issues.

effects.label: Metabolic Stress:
effects.description: Most effective when the body is under high stress, such as post-surgery or during heavy training.

effects.label: Performance Gains:
effects.description: While excellent for recovery, it shows limited direct impact on increasing maximum strength or speed.

effects.label: Clinical Utility:
effects.description: Proven effectiveness in accelerating the healing of chronic wounds and burns.

dosage.label: Standard Daily Dose:
dosage.description: Common reported dosages for gut health range from 5g to 15g daily, often split into multiple doses.

dosage.label: High Stress/Clinical:
dosage.description: Dosages in clinical burn studies or for severe gut repairs can exceed 20g-30g per day under supervision.

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Best taken on an empty stomach or immediately following a workout for optimal absorption.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Upset:
sideEffects.description: Excessive doses can occasionally lead to constipation or mild bloating in sensitive individuals.

sideEffects.label: Neurological Sensitivity:
sideEffects.description: Rare reports of increased anxiety or 'brain fog' in individuals sensitive to glutamate conversion.

sideEffects.label: Water Retention:
sideEffects.description: Some users report a minor increase in intramuscular water retention.

availability.label: Over the Counter:
availability.description: Readily available as an inexpensive powder or capsule at most health food stores and pharmacies.

availability.label: Purity:
availability.description: Widely available in fermented (vegan) or synthetic forms with high levels of purity (L-Glutamine).
